summary: All in-scope round-1 findings from commit 05c9732 are addressed correctly. M1 is resolved with a synchronous Send-gate flip in commitDraft and a matching revert on failure; m2 removes the redundant observer and correctly relies on the combine in buildMessagesFlow plus stop()'s interrupted-bubble handling; m3 collapses to a single finally-driven `_reinitInProgress = false`; m4 replaces `first { it != null }!!` with `mapNotNull { it }.first()`. M2 (route heavy apply through WarmupCoordinator.cancelAndRestart) is explicitly deferred with a documented rationale tied to Task 5 coordinator contract work and is accepted. Two stale doc references to the removed observePersistedForHandover remain (cosmetic, tracked as minor).

findings.title: Draft Send-gate flipped synchronously, revert on failure present
findings.detail: `commitDraft` now sets `_uiState.value = ChatUiState.Ready(isGenerating = true)` at line 548 BEFORE `viewModelScope.launch`, after the `modelId`/`currentReadyModel()` guards. The failure branch at line 577 reverts to `Ready(isGenerating = false)` alongside the error log and snackbar, restoring retry. A rapid double-tap's second `send()` will now observe `isGenerating = true` at the outer gate (line 174) and return immediately. Success path leaves the flag `true` so Quick/Persistent can't race â€” the Persistent VM that loads on nav will re-initialize its own state from the engine observer.

findings.title: applyHeavySetting still calls registry.cleanup + registry.initialize directly â€” deferral accepted
findings.detail: Lines 325-326 still bypass `WarmupCoordinator.cancelAndRestart`. Commit message and decisions.md acknowledge this: the current coordinator.cancelAndRestart does not perform an explicit cleanup pass, and Task 8's sequencing tests assert `stopResponse â†’ cleanup â†’ initialize` on the registry. A full migration requires Task 5 coordinator contract work. The tech-spec lifecycleMutex risk remains but is an inherited constraint, not a Task 8 regression. Accepting as deferred with the documented tracking in decisions.md.
findings.recommendation: Track in a follow-up referencing Task 5 coordinator contract; no block on Task 8.

findings.title: Draft staged attachments deferral â€” out of scope per task
findings.detail: `@Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER") val attachmentsPlaceholder = pending` at line 544; commit clears `_attachments` on success. Deferral acknowledged in decisions.md. No change requested per round-2 scope.

findings.title: observePersistedForHandover removed â€” combine is the sole invariant
findings.detail: The init-block launch and the function itself are gone. `buildMessagesFlow` (line 484) still suppresses `streaming` when `persisted.lastOrNull()?.role == ROLE_ASSISTANT`, which is the load-bearing invariant for D4. `stop()` (line 184-200) marks the streaming bubble as `interrupted=true` (keeping it visible, not nulled) â€” correct for AC-R3 since no ASSISTANT row is persisted on stop, so the combine won't hide it, and the user still sees the interrupted partial. Non-Persistent modes use `updateLastAssistantInMemory` and are unaffected. `resetConversation` and `applyHeavySetting`'s prior-streaming clear still null `_streamingMessage` explicitly where appropriate.

findings.title: applyHeavySetting relies solely on finally for _reinitInProgress reset
findings.detail: Both early-return assignments removed (lines 305 and 320 now use `?: return@launch` without a side-assignment); the `finally` at line 343-345 is the single writer for `_reinitInProgress.value = false` and covers all exit paths (early return, exception in cleanup/initialize, onSuccess, onFailure). Verified no remaining writes in the try body.

findings.title: mapNotNull { it }.first() replaces first { it != null }!!
findings.detail: `mapNotNull` import added at line 38; line 475 now reads `registry.activeModelName.mapNotNull { it }.first()` returning a non-null `String` without the bang. Readability improved; semantics identical.

findings.title: Stale KDoc references to removed observePersistedForHandover
findings.detail: Two comments still mention the removed function: line 126 (`cleared by the side-effect collector in [observePersistedForHandover]`) inside the `messages` KDoc, and line 488 (`collector in [observePersistedForHandover] has not yet fired`) inside `buildMessagesFlow`. Since the side-effect collector no longer exists, these KDoc links dangle and the narrative is now incorrect â€” the combine is the sole mechanism, not a defence-in-depth over a collector. Not a functional issue but misleading for future readers.
findings.recommendation: Update both comments to describe the combine as the single invariant; remove the `[observePersistedForHandover]` references. Can be rolled into the next cosmetic touch-up.
